name: Sierra Espuña gravel tour
description:
Sierra Espuña hides in the most unknown part of Murcia, famous for its beaches, almost in the province of Almería. The Route takes you around the limestone massifs and runs through impressive gypsum ravines where erosion creates whimsical shapes, ascends in the shade of the trees and takes you to the heights revealing wonderful panoramic views.

The route is circular from the Sierra Espuña campsite. The best way to get here is by private vehicle and you can leave it parked without any problem. The itinerary avoids narrow trails, but climbs some mountain roads. To complete it safely, it is advisable that your gravel bike has resistant tires.

The Route immerses you in the depths of these mountains and does not include passing through any town, so we recommend that you bring with you enough water and food and some light spare parts that will allow you to complete it without setbacks. The seasons with the friendliest temperatures and best light to contemplate the landscape are spring and autumn.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Alhama De Murcia offers diverse topography, from mountain ranges to striking badlands, providing varied experiences for cyclists. The region is characterized by well-maintained gravel roads that wind through pine forests, particularly within the Sierra Espuña Regional Park. Cyclists can navigate a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces across rugged, pine-covered mountains and unique geological formations. This landscape provides a range of routes suitable for different fitness levels. Best gravel bike trails around Alhama De Murcia The most popular gravel bike trail is Sierra Espuña gravel tour, a 65.7 miles (105.7 km) trail that takes 10 hours 48 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the extensive Sierra Espuña Regional Park, known for its forested mountains. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Espubinc Visitor Center – Leiva Valley Trail loop from Cerro del Cristo, a difficult 16.9 miles (27.2 km) path. This route explores the scenic Leiva Valley, offering varied terrain and views. Local gravel bikers also love the Leiva Valley Trail – Green land in sight loop from Alhama de Murcia, a 46.6 miles (74.9 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes, often completed in about 7 hours 6 minutes. Gravel biking around Alhama De Murcia is defined by pine-covered mountains, dramatic badlands, and deep ravines. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Alhama De Murcia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from a few reviews. More than 80 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Alhama De Murcia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails around Alhama De Murcia?
A: The region offers a diverse mix of landscapes. You'll encounter well-maintained gravel roads winding through the pine forests of the Sierra Espuña Regional Park, as well as rugged, pine-covered mountains and dramatic badlands like the Barrancos de Gebas. Expect a blend of paved and unpaved surfaces, providing varied and engaging experiences.

Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in the Alhama De Murcia area?
A: There are over 25 gravel bike trails around Alhama De Murcia, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored many of these routes, providing valuable insights.

Q: Are there any easy gravel bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: While many routes in the area are rated as difficult, there are options for varying fitness levels. For a less strenuous experience, look for routes that stick to the flatter sections or less technical paths within the Sierra Espuña. Always check the route details for elevation and terrain before you go.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can see on these gravel routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views. You can explore the rugged, pine-covered mountains of the Sierra Espuña Regional Park, or witness the unique, almost lunar landscape of the Barrancos de Gebas with its striking colors. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4371651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla de Algeciras Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; also provides a picturesque backdrop on some trails.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit along the gravel bike trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/704570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morrón de Alhama Summit&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, and the region itself is rich in history, with the town of Alhama de Murcia featuring an Arab castle and an Archaeological Museum. The Parish Church of San Lazaro Bishop is another emblematic monument.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Alhama De Murcia?
A: The mild, dry winters in Murcia make it an attractive year-round cycling destination, offering a quieter alternative to more northern training grounds. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant with moderate temperatures, while summer can be very hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the gravel routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espubinc Visitor Center – Leiva Valley Trail loop from Cerro del Cristo&lt;/a&gt; explores the scenic Leiva Valley, offering varied terrain and views in a circular format.

Q: Where can I find parking for gravel biking in Alhama De Murcia?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Alhama de Murcia, especially near trailheads or in villages like El Berro, which serves as a quiet base for exploring Sierra Espuña. Specific parking details can often be found in the route descriptions on komoot.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about the trails in Alhama De Murcia?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse topography, from the lush mountain ranges to the striking badlands, and the well-maintained gravel roads that wind through pine forests, particularly within the Sierra Espuña Regional Park.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the gravel bike trails?
A: Yes, you'll find options, especially in the villages and towns surrounding the Sierra Espuña. The Fuente del Hilo restaurant within Sierra Espuña Regional Park is known as a popular cyclist hangout. El Berro, a quaint hamlet, also offers amenities for cyclists.

Q: What is a challenging long-distance gravel route in the region?
A: For a significant challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1497923568/sierra-espuna-gravel-tour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Espuña gravel tour&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult route spans over 105 km and traverses the extensive Sierra Espuña Regional Park, known for its forested mountains and substantial elevation changes.

Q: Can I find routes that feature the Tajo-Segura Transfer Canal?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate this notable feature. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo-Segura Transfer Canal – Collado de Las Chaparras loop from Moriana&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that includes sections along the canal, offering unique scenery.
tips: The path of water is a path that changes width. Some sections are narrow paths and some are track. The route takes place under the pleasant shade of the pine forest and at some points it has spectacular views.

The landscape of the basin that feeds the Algeciras boulevard seems lunar. This barren terrain, known as "badlands", is a gypsum landscape where erosion has shaped capricious pale-colored slopes and gullies.

The Humero pass, at an altitude of 1,350 metres, is one of the most iconic places in Sierra Espuña. From here, between limestone cliffs, you get an impressive view of the surrounding bluish ridges and the cultivated fields of the lower area.
This is the alternative track climb to Collado Bermejo perfect for gravel or MTB, it is about 9 kilometers and well compacted terrain.
This steep concrete track connects the Blanco pass and has incredible views of the green blanket of the pine forest broken by the masses of limestone.

Hidden between the peaks of Sierra Espuña, Prado Mayor is a tiny valley that you can descend from Collado Blanco (1,223 meters), at the head of the Leiva Valley. It offers good views and the gift of absolute silence.
